<p>Implementing the <code>print_usage()</code> func in both Go and Rust is very simple and is done in the <code>main()</code> func respectively:<br>
</p>

<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="n">main</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Args</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="m">2</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">printUsage</span><span class="p">()</span>
        <span class="k">return</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">main</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{</span>

    <span class="c1">// .collect() returns the .args() into a Vec<String> format</span>
    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">Vec</span><span class="o"><</span><span class="nb">String</span><span class="o">></span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">env</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">args</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="nf">.collect</span><span class="p">();</span>

    <span class="c1">// ensure that there are enough arguments to run the program</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="nf">.len</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="mi">2</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="nf">print_usage</span><span class="p">();</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>The big difference here is the <strong>Go</strong> <code>os.Args</code> and the <strong>Rust</strong> <code>std::env::args().collect()</code>. Clearly, Rust is more verbose, and without <code>.collect()</code> the value of the <code>.args()</code> could be a tuple of <code><String, Err></code>, but <code>.collect()</code> returns a <code>Vec<String></code> if there are no <code>Err</code> thrown.</p>

<p>With this basic program functionality, we can implement in the <code>main()</code> func in both languages what that <em>switching</em> is going to look like parsing the 2nd and 3rd arguments respectively.<br>
</p>

<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="n">main</span><span class="p">(){</span> 
    <span class="c">// ... existing</span>

    <span class="k">switch</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="m">1</span><span class="p">]</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="c">// 1. taskr search <query></span>
        <span class="k">case</span> <span class="s">"search"</span><span class="o">:</span>

        <span class="c">// 2. taskr add <title></span>
        <span class="k">case</span> <span class="s">"add"</span><span class="o">:</span>

        <span class="c">// 3. taskr list</span>
        <span class="k">case</span> <span class="s">"list"</span><span class="o">:</span>

        <span class="c">// 4. taskr done <id></span>
        <span class="k">case</span> <span class="s">"done"</span><span class="o">:</span>

        <span class="c">// 5. taskr delete <id></span>
        <span class="k">case</span> <span class="s">"delete"</span><span class="o">:</span>

        <span class="c">// 6. taskr <else> catch-all</span>
        <span class="k">default</span><span class="o">:</span>

    <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="c">//- end of main</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">main</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="c1">// ... existing</span>

    <span class="k">match</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="nf">.as_str</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{</span>

        <span class="c1">// 1. taskr search <query></span>
        <span class="s">"search"</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="p">{}</span>

        <span class="c1">// 2. taskr add <title></span>
        <span class="s">"add"</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="p">{}</span>

        <span class="c1">// 3. taskr list</span>
        <span class="s">"list"</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="p">{}</span>

        <span class="c1">// 4. taskr done <id></span>
        <span class="s">"done"</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="p">{}</span>

        <span class="c1">// 5. taskr delete <id></span>
        <span class="s">"delete"</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="p">{}</span>

        <span class="c1">// 6. taskr <else> catch-all</span>
        <span class="n">_</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="p">{</span>
           <span class="nd">eprintln!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Invalid command"</span><span class="p">);</span>
            <span class="nf">print_usage</span><span class="p">();</span>
            <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">process</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">);</span> <span class="c1">// SIGTERM 1</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="p">}</span> <span class="c1">//- match arg[1]</span>

    <span class="c1">//- end of main</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>The <code>match</code> (rust) and <code>switch</code> (go) statements are both reading from <code>args[1]</code>. The <code>0</code> in <code>args[0]</code> would be the filename of the binary running. In the example of <strong>taskr list</strong>, the <code>args[0]</code> is <code>taskr</code> and <code>args[1]</code> is <code>list</code>. In <strong>rust</strong>, the <code>.as_str()</code> is used to ensure that the underlying type is a string, since the <code>statement</code> is a <code>String</code> in <code>"statement" => { }</code> (rust) and <code>case "statement":</code> (go). </p>

<p>Next, we are going to be implementing the <code>TaskStore</code>, but before we do that, we need to ensure that our programs respectively have the correct imports.<br>
</p>

<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">import</span> <span class="p">(</span>
    <span class="s">"encoding/json"</span>
    <span class="s">"errors"</span>
    <span class="s">"fmt"</span>
    <span class="s">"os"</span>
    <span class="s">"strconv"</span>
    <span class="s">"strings"</span>
<span class="p">)</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="c1">// serde is used for JSON structure support at compile time</span>
<span class="k">use</span> <span class="nn">serde</span><span class="p">::{</span><span class="n">Deserialize</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">Serialize</span><span class="p">};</span>

<span class="c1">// read and write on file functions</span>
<span class="k">use</span> <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="n">fs</span><span class="p">;</span>

<span class="c1">// access the io::Result type for propagating I/O errors</span>
<span class="k">use</span> <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="n">io</span><span class="p">;</span>

<span class="c1">// owned mutable file path</span>
<span class="k">use</span> <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">path</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="n">PathBuf</span><span class="p">;</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>These imports allow each of the programs to respectively handle the functionality needed. Within <strong>Go</strong>, you'll interact with the <code>os</code> package for I/O related work and in <strong>Rust</strong>, you interact with <code>std::io</code> instead. Files are handled in <strong>Go</strong> using the <code>os</code> package, as well as the <code>fs</code> and <code>ioutil</code> packages. <strong>Rust</strong> consolidates it into a two packages called <code>std::io</code> and <code>std::fs</code>. Both languages are designed to be cross platform natively. One last thing to point out about this is the <code>std::path::PathBuf</code> and how its very similar to Go's <code>filepath</code> package offering. In Rust, the <code>PathBuf</code> type is specifically for filesystem paths. On Windows, you split your directory tree using <code>\</code> and on Linux, you split your directory tree using <code>/</code>. Those differences are handled under the hood by the <code>filepath</code> (Go) and <code>std::path::PathBuf</code> (Rust) automatically. </p>

<h2>
  
  
  The Task Store
</h2>

<p>The bulk of the logic of the application is going to expose <code>TaskStore</code> in both <strong>Go</strong> and <strong>Rust</strong>. How these are implemented matter and you'll see why that is.<br>
</p>

<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">type</span> <span class="n">TaskStore</span> <span class="k">struct</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="n">tasks</span>    <span class="p">[]</span><span class="n">Task</span>
    <span class="n">filePath</span> <span class="kt">string</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">struct</span> <span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">Vec</span><span class="o"><</span><span class="n">Task</span><span class="o">></span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="n">file_path</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="n">PathBuf</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>Both of these snippets show you how similar <strong>Go</strong> and <strong>Rust</strong> can appear. The <code>[]Task</code> in <strong>Go</strong> becomes a <code>Vec<Task></code> in <strong>Rust</strong> and the <code>filePath</code> uses <strong>Rust</strong>'s <code>PathBuf</code>. In <strong>Go</strong>, the <code>struct</code> can use and store the <code>filePath</code> as a <code>string</code> since <code>filepath</code> package returns only <code>String</code> types back - <strong>Rust</strong> gives you an entirely new type called <code>PathBuf</code> whereas <strong>Go</strong> gives you a wrapper for it.</p>

<p>When implementing the methods on the <code>TaskStore</code>, this is where <strong>Go</strong> and <strong>Rust</strong> diverge quite radically.<br>
</p>

<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="n">NewTaskStore</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">filePath</span> <span class="kt">string</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="kt">error</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{}</span>

<span class="k">func</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="n">Save</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="kt">error</span> <span class="p">{}</span>

<span class="k">func</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="n">Add</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">title</span> <span class="kt">string</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{}</span>

<span class="k">func</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="n">Complete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">id</span> <span class="kt">uint32</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="kt">error</span> <span class="p">{}</span>

<span class="k">func</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="n">Delete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">id</span> <span class="kt">uint32</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="kt">error</span> <span class="p">{}</span>

<span class="k">func</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="n">List</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{}</span>

<span class="k">func</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="n">Search</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">query</span> <span class="kt">string</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">[]</span><span class="o">*</span><span class="n">Task</span> <span class="p">{}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">impl</span> <span class="n">TaskStore</span> <span class="p">{</span>

    <span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">new</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">file_path</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="n">PathBuf</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">-></span> <span class="nn">io</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nb">Result</span><span class="o"><</span><span class="k">Self</span><span class="o">></span> <span class="p">{}</span>

    <span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">save</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="k">self</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">-></span> <span class="nn">io</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nb">Result</span><span class="o"><</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="o">></span> <span class="p">{}</span>

    <span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">add</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="k">mut</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">title</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">String</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{}</span>

    <span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">complete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="k">mut</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">u32</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">-></span> <span class="nb">Result</span><span class="o"><</span><span class="p">(),</span> <span class="nb">String</span><span class="o">></span> <span class="p">{}</span>

    <span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">delete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="k">mut</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">u32</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">-></span> <span class="nb">Result</span><span class="o"><</span><span class="p">(),</span> <span class="nb">String</span><span class="o">></span> <span class="p">{}</span>

    <span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">list</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="k">self</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{}</span>

    <span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">search</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="k">self</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">query</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="o">&</span><span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">-></span> <span class="nb">Vec</span><span class="o"><&</span><span class="n">Task</span><span class="o">></span> <span class="p">{}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>There is a pretty clean <code>1:1</code> relationship between the implemented methods of the <code>TaskStore</code> between <strong>Go</strong> and <strong>Rust</strong> and specifically what I want to note here for you is how in <strong>Rust</strong> everything is wrapped inside of the <code>impl TaskStore {}</code> block itself, whereas in Go, this can be literally anywhere at the root level of the file inside the <code>package main</code> project. The order of the routes does not matter in either language.</p>

<p><strong>Rust</strong> has a convention called <code>io::Result<Self></code> which in <strong>Go</strong> simply looks like <code>(<Type>, error)</code>. The <code>NewTaskStore</code> method in <strong>Go</strong> is unattached per-se to the <code>TaskStore</code> <code>struct</code> itself, but it does return the <strong>Go</strong> equivalent of <code>(*TaskStore, error)</code> as <code>io::Result<Self></code>. When <code>io::Result<Self></code> is used, the method must also include <code>Ok(())</code> in order to satisfy the contract.</p>

<p>At this point, let's ensure that our <strong>Go</strong> program has two little helper functions in it in order to improve runtime readability - this is common in Go.<br>
</p>

<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="n">parseID</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">arg</span> <span class="kt">string</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="kt">uint32</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="n">strconv</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ParseUint</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">arg</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="m">10</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="m">32</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">!=</span> <span class="no">nil</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Fprintf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Stderr</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s">"Invalid task ID: %s</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">arg</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="m">1</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="k">return</span> <span class="kt">uint32</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">id</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="p">}</span>

<span class="k">func</span> <span class="n">mustSave</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Save</span><span class="p">();</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">!=</span> <span class="no">nil</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Fprintf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Stderr</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s">"Error saving tasks: %v</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">err</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="m">1</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>This application does not need or use helper functions in the <strong>Rust</strong> implementation. In <strong>Rust</strong> we're using <code>u32</code> for the <code>id</code> and in <strong>Go</strong> that is a <code>uint32</code> which imports the <code>strconv</code> package. <strong>Rust</strong> does not need to import any packages. </p>

<p>Before implementing the specifics in the <code>TaskStore</code>, lets consume it in the <code>main()</code> func.<br>
</p>

<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="n">main</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{</span>

    <span class="c">// ... after checking the length of args</span>

    <span class="n">store</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="n">NewTaskStore</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"tasks.json"</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">!=</span> <span class="no">nil</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Fprintf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Stderr</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s">"Error loading tasks: %v</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">err</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="m">1</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="c">// ... before switching args[1]</span>

<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">main</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="k">-></span> <span class="nn">io</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nb">Result</span><span class="o"><</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="o">></span> <span class="p">{</span>

    <span class="c1">// ... after checking the length of args</span>

    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">database</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="nn">PathBuf</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">from</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"tasks.json"</span><span class="p">);</span>

    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="k">mut</span> <span class="n">store</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="k">match</span> <span class="nn">TaskStore</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">new</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">database</span><span class="p">){</span>
        <span class="nf">Ok</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">store</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="n">store</span><span class="p">,</span>
        <span class="nf">Err</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">e</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="p">{</span>
            <span class="nd">eprintln!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Error loading tasks: {}"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">e</span><span class="p">);</span>
            <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">process</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">);</span> <span class="c1">// SIGTERM 1</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="p">};</span>

    <span class="c1">// ... before matching args[1]</span>

<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>Both of these implementations will leverage the <strong>current working directory</strong> and consume the resource <code>tasks.json</code> if it exists. If it does not exist, the program will create it, if it exists, the program will overwrite it. </p>

<p>In <strong>Rust</strong>, the <code>TaskStore</code> is consumed slightly differently than in <strong>Go</strong> because of the <code>impl TaskStore {}</code> differences. In <strong>Rust</strong> the <code>TaskStore</code> must have <code>mut</code> after <code>let</code> and before the name of the variable in order to indicate to the compiler that the value of <code>store</code> is eligible for changing in the future. Since the <code>TaskStore::new(database)</code> returns with an <code>io::Result<Self></code>, we can <code>match</code> against it when assigning it to the <code>store</code> variable. </p>

<p>The <strong>Go</strong> tuple that <code>io::Result<Self></code> is satisfying is <code>(*TaskStore, error)</code> and in <strong>Rust</strong>, the first component <code>*TaskStore</code> is represented in by the <code>Ok()</code> invocation. At the end of the various <code>impl TaskStore {}</code> methods, you'll see an <code>Ok(())</code> at the end of each method ; this invokes the contract and exits the method since we're good. Otherwise, your program will not compile. When assigning a value to <code>store</code> and executing <code>TaskStore::new(database)</code>, each component of the output gets matched to the <code>Ok(store) =></code> or <code>Err(e) => {}</code> blocks respectively. When we receive an <code>Ok(())</code> then that returned <code>store</code> from <code>Ok()</code> is then assigned to the <code>let mut store</code> variable. </p>

<p>Otherwise, if <code>Err(e)</code> satisfies, it means that <em>something went wrong</em> and we'll print the error and exit with status code 1. In <strong>Go</strong>, we use <code>os.Exit(1)</code> and in <strong>Rust</strong> we use <code>std::process::exit(1);</code>. In <strong>Go</strong>, we can use the <code>fmt</code> or <code>slog</code> or <a href="https://github.com/andreimerlescu/verbose" rel="noopener noreferrer">verbose</a> packages to write to STDOUT and STDERR, and in <strong>Rust</strong> we use <code>eprintln!()</code> for STDERR and <code>println!()</code> for STDOUT respectively.</p>

<h2>
  
  
  Implementing <strong>new</strong> <code>TaskStore</code>
</h2>

We're going to implement the <code>TaskStore</code> now, and that means populating each of the methods in TWO locations. First in the <code>impl TaskStore {}</code> block for <strong>Rust</strong> and inside of the <code>func (s *TaskStore) ...</code> methods for <strong>Go</strong>. </p>

<h3>
  
  
  TaskStore's <code>new</code> Implemented
</h3>



<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="n">NewTaskStore</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">filePath</span> <span class="kt">string</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="kt">error</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="n">tasks</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="p">[]</span><span class="n">Task</span><span class="p">{}</span>

    <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ReadFile</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">filePath</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="no">nil</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">jsonErr</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="n">json</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Unmarshal</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="o">&</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">);</span> <span class="n">jsonErr</span> <span class="o">!=</span> <span class="no">nil</span> <span class="p">{</span>
            <span class="n">tasks</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[]</span><span class="n">Task</span><span class="p">{}</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="p">}</span> <span class="k">else</span> <span class="k">if</span> <span class="o">!</span><span class="n">errors</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Is</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">err</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ErrNotExist</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="k">return</span> <span class="no">nil</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">err</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="k">return</span> <span class="o">&</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">{</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="o">:</span> <span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">filePath</span><span class="o">:</span> <span class="n">filePath</span><span class="p">},</span> <span class="no">nil</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">new</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">file_path</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="n">PathBuf</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">-></span> <span class="nn">io</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nb">Result</span><span class="o"><</span><span class="k">Self</span><span class="o">></span> <span class="p">{</span>

    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">tasks</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">file_path</span><span class="nf">.exists</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">data</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="nn">fs</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">read_to_string</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="n">file_path</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="o">?</span><span class="p">;</span>
        <span class="nn">serde_json</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">from_str</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="n">data</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="nf">.unwrap_or_else</span><span class="p">(|</span><span class="n">_</span><span class="p">|</span> <span class="nn">Vec</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">new</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span> <span class="k">else</span> <span class="p">{</span> 
        <span class="nn">Vec</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">new</span><span class="p">()</span>
    <span class="p">};</span>

    <span class="nf">Ok</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="k">Self</span> <span class="p">{</span> <span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">file_path</span> <span class="p">})</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>The most notable thing to understand in this code block is the difference between how <strong>Go</strong> and <strong>Rust</strong> respectively handle <strong>JSON</strong>. In <strong>Go</strong>, you can use the <code>json.Unmarshal</code> method to parse the string of data and map it into a struct - the compiler will let you break everything - by design. <strong>Rust</strong> on the other hand, blocks it entirely before you can even compile. What does this mean? Instead of handing off the task to the <em>go runtime</em> in order to determine the <em>types</em> of the fields and how they are to map from string form into structured data form - <strong>Rust</strong> does it before compilation time using <code>.unwrap_or_else</code> whereas <strong>Go</strong> relies on <code>jsonErr != nil</code> to understand if a problem occurred. <strong>Rust</strong> enforces the types of the <code>struct</code> that you're mapping data into at compilation time, whereas <strong>Go</strong> attempts to do this at runtime. This difference could be spending 5x the amount of time you'd normally spend in <strong>Go</strong> running <code>Unmarshal</code> on the data getting the type explicit nature of <strong>Rust</strong> compiling correctly. Ultimately, <strong>Rust</strong> forces correctness before compilation time - preventing the most painful part of <strong>Go</strong> development - <em>interacting with irregular JSON files</em>. </p>

<p>Literally, an <em>irregular JSON file</em> being implemented in <strong>Rust</strong> could legitimately take you 5x the amount of time to get right. The investment of time in this, on the surface, feels like its a waste and unnecessary - but years down the road, when you re-compile your program and <strong>Rust</strong> will always have correctness built in, whereas at any point that <strong>Go</strong> changes the <code>json</code> package, the way the runtime determines <em>type</em> <strong>could</strong> change. Doesn't mean that it will or is wants to - but it could. And that potential, matters when you're choosing a language that you can write once, and compile 25 years later with zero effort. C gives that to you. C++ gives that to you. Rust gives that to you. Go tries to. Higher level languages like Python, PHP and Ruby - can't by definition and design of their languages. </p>

<p>We consumed the <code>new</code> method in the <code>main()</code> func when we established our <code>let mut store</code> (rust) and <code>store, err := NewTaskStore("tasks.json")</code> (go). </p>

<h3>
  
  
  TaskStore's <code>list</code> Implemented
</h3>



<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="n">List</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="m">0</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Println</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"No tasks yet. Add one with: taskr add </span><span class="se">\"</span><span class="s">your task</span><span class="se">\"</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="k">return</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">_</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="k">range</span> <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">marker</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="s">"[ ]"</span>
        <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Status</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="n">StatusDone</span> <span class="p">{</span>
            <span class="n">marker</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="s">"[X]"</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Printf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"%s %d - %s</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">marker</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ID</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Title</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">list</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="k">self</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="py">.tasks</span><span class="nf">.is_empty</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="nd">println!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"No tasks yet. Add one with: taskr add </span><span class="se">\"</span><span class="s">your task</span><span class="se">\"</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">);</span>
        <span class="k">return</span><span class="p">;</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">task</span> <span class="k">in</span> <span class="o">&</span><span class="k">self</span><span class="py">.tasks</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">market</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="k">match</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="py">.status</span> <span class="p">{</span>
            <span class="nn">Status</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="n">Todo</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="s">"[ ]"</span><span class="p">,</span>
            <span class="nn">Status</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="n">Done</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="s">"[X]"</span><span class="p">,</span>
        <span class="p">};</span>
        <span class="nd">println!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"{} {} - {}"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">market</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="py">.id</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="py">.title</span><span class="p">);</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>Respectively, these two methods look pretty much the same. Specifically in <strong>Rust</strong> we're leveraging the borrower for <code>&self.tasks</code> and in <strong>Go</strong> we just range over them without needing to indicate memory usage. Without the <code>Debug</code> derived decorator attacked to the <code>Task</code> struct, the <code>println!()</code> methods that use <code>{}</code> wouldn't compile.</p>

<h3>
  
  
  TaskStore's <code>add</code> Implemented
</h3>



<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="n">Add</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">title</span> <span class="kt">string</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">var</span> <span class="n">maxID</span> <span class="kt">uint32</span>
    <span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">_</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">t</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="k">range</span> <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">t</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ID</span> <span class="o">></span> <span class="n">maxID</span> <span class="p">{</span>
            <span class="n">maxID</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">t</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ID</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="n">task</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="n">Task</span><span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">ID</span><span class="o">:</span>     <span class="n">maxID</span> <span class="o">+</span> <span class="m">1</span><span class="p">,</span>
        <span class="n">Title</span><span class="o">:</span>  <span class="n">title</span><span class="p">,</span>
        <span class="n">Status</span><span class="o">:</span> <span class="n">StatusTodo</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Printf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Added task %d: %s</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ID</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Title</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="nb">append</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">add</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="k">mut</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">title</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">String</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">id</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="py">.tasks</span><span class="nf">.iter</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="c1">// use the iterator</span>
        <span class="nf">.map</span><span class="p">(|</span><span class="n">t</span><span class="p">|</span> <span class="n">t</span><span class="py">.id</span><span class="p">)</span>         <span class="c1">// use only the id field</span>
        <span class="nf">.max</span><span class="p">()</span>                 <span class="c1">// select the maximum value in the set</span>
        <span class="nf">.unwrap_or</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o">+</span> <span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">;</span>     <span class="c1">// if no err, use # else use 0 + 1</span>

    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">task</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">Task</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">,</span>                    <span class="c1">// short for `id: id,`</span>
        <span class="n">title</span><span class="p">,</span>                 <span class="c1">// short for `title: title,`</span>
        <span class="n">status</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nn">Status</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="n">Todo</span><span class="p">,</span>
    <span class="p">};</span>

    <span class="nd">println!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Added task {}: {}"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="py">.id</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="py">.title</span><span class="p">);</span>

    <span class="k">self</span><span class="py">.tasks</span><span class="nf">.push</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">task</span><span class="p">);</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>Most notably between this <strong>Go</strong> and <strong>Rust</strong> code you can see the manner for acquiring the <em>next ID</em> in the set is radically different. In <strong>Rust</strong> we are using the <code>.iter()</code> in order to <code>.map()</code> then <code>.max()</code> with a final check of <code>.unwrap_or(0)</code> in order to get an iterable list of IDs via the <code>.map()</code> extraction of just the <code>id</code> field, followed by the <code>.max()</code> choice. This quickly returns the largest integer of the result and then attempts to <code>+ 1</code> at the end of it. In <strong>Rust</strong> this expression is a simple statement and in <strong>Go</strong> its multiple lines using <code>maxID</code> inside the method. </p>

<p>In <strong>Rust</strong> without the <code>&mut</code> on the <code>self</code> argument of the method, the <code>self.tasks.push(task);</code> would fail because the compiler is missing the <code>&mut</code> on <code>self</code> in order to actually <em>write</em> via <code>.push()</code>. In <strong>Go</strong>, we simply use <code>append(...)</code>. </p>

<p>Both languages now need to take this <code>impl TaskStore { "add" => {} }</code> and <code>func (s *TaskStore) Add(title string)</code> and consume them in the <code>main()</code> func.<br>
</p>

<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="n">main</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="c">// ... existing after \`switch args[1]\`</span>

    <span class="k">case</span> <span class="s">"add"</span><span class="o">:</span>
        <span class="k">if</span> <span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">args</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="m">3</span> <span class="p">{</span>
            <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Fprintln</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Stderr</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s">"Usage: taskr add <title>"</span><span class="p">)</span>
            <span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="m">1</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>
        <span class="n">store</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Add</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">strings</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Join</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="m">2</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="s">" "</span><span class="p">))</span>
        <span class="n">mustSave</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">store</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="c">// ... existing before \`default:\` case </span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">main</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{</span>

    <span class="c1">// ... existing after `match args[1].to_str() {`</span>

    <span class="c1">// 2. taskr add <title></span>
    <span class="s">"add"</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="nf">.len</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="mi">3</span> <span class="p">{</span>
            <span class="nd">eprintln!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Usage: taskr add <title>"</span><span class="p">);</span>
            <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">process</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">);</span> <span class="c1">// SIGTERM 1</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>
        <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">title</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="o">..</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="nf">.join</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">" "</span><span class="p">);</span>
        <span class="n">store</span><span class="nf">.add</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">title</span><span class="p">);</span>
        <span class="n">store</span><span class="nf">.save</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="o">?</span><span class="p">;</span>
        <span class="nf">Ok</span><span class="p">(())</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="c1">// ... existing before `_ => {}` match</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>Our <strong>Go</strong> code is consuming the <code>mustSave</code> helper method and our <strong>Rust</strong> code is calling <code>.add()</code> and <code>.save()</code> endpoints on the <code>TaskStore</code> <code>impl</code>. </p>

<h3>
  
  
  TaskStore's <code>delete</code> Implemented
</h3>



<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="n">Delete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">id</span> <span class="kt">uint32</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="kt">error</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">i</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">t</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="k">range</span> <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">t</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ID</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="n">id</span> <span class="p">{</span>
            <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Printf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Removed task %d: %s</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">t</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ID</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">t</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Title</span><span class="p">)</span>
            <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="nb">append</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="o">+</span><span class="m">1</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">...</span><span class="p">)</span>
            <span class="k">return</span> <span class="no">nil</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="k">return</span> <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Errorf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"task %d not found"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">delete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="k">mut</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">u32</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">-></span> <span class="nb">Result</span><span class="o"><</span><span class="p">(),</span> <span class="nb">String</span><span class="o">></span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">pos</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="py">.tasks</span><span class="nf">.iter</span><span class="p">()</span>       <span class="c1">// get an iterator</span>
        <span class="nf">.position</span><span class="p">(|</span><span class="n">t</span><span class="p">|</span> <span class="n">t</span><span class="py">.id</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">)</span>     <span class="c1">// find the index of the id</span>
        <span class="nf">.ok_or_else</span><span class="p">(||</span> 
            <span class="nd">format!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Task {} not found"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="p">)</span><span class="o">?</span><span class="p">;</span>

    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">removed</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="py">.tasks</span><span class="nf">.remove</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">pos</span><span class="p">);</span>
    <span class="nd">println!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Removed task {}: {}"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">removed</span><span class="py">.id</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">removed</span><span class="py">.title</span><span class="p">);</span> 
    <span class="nf">Ok</span><span class="p">(())</span> 
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>As you can see - these methods look so <em>similar</em> and that's because <strong>Go</strong> and <strong>Rust</strong> compliment each other, like <strong>C</strong> and <strong>C++</strong> complimented each other. They are designed to be similar, but one for more lower level work and the other for more higher level data driven work.  Either way, when you implement these methods, you see their implementations even look similar.<br>
</p>

<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">case</span> <span class="s">"delete"</span><span class="o">:</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">args</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="m">3</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Fprintln</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Stderr</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s">"Usage: taskr delete <id>"</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="m">1</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="n">store</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Delete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">parseID</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="m">2</span><span class="p">]));</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">!=</span> <span class="no">nil</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Fprintln</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Stderr</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">err</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="m">1</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="n">mustSave</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">store</span><span class="p">)</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="s">"delete"</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="nf">.len</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="mi">3</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="nd">eprintln!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Usage: taskr delete"</span><span class="p">);</span>
        <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">process</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">);</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">u32</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="nf">.parse</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="nf">.unwrap_or_else</span><span class="p">(|</span><span class="n">_</span><span class="p">|</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="nd">eprintln!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Invalid task ID: {}"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">]);</span>
        <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">process</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">);</span>
    <span class="p">});</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="k">let</span> <span class="nf">Err</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">e</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">store</span><span class="nf">.delete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">id</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="nd">eprintln!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"{}"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">e</span><span class="p">);</span>
        <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">process</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">);</span> <span class="c1">// SIGTERM 1</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="n">store</span><span class="nf">.save</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="o">?</span><span class="p">;</span>
    <span class="nf">Ok</span><span class="p">(())</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>Most notably in the differences between these two implementations, you can see the <strong>Go</strong> helper method <code>parseID</code> being used here where <strong>Rust</strong> just leverages <code>.parse().unwrap_or_else(|_| {} )</code> instead. </p>

<h3>
  
  
  TaskStore's <code>complete</code> | <code>done</code> Implemented
</h3>



<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="n">Complete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">id</span> <span class="kt">uint32</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="kt">error</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">i</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="k">range</span> <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ID</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="n">id</span> <span class="p">{</span>
            <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Status</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">StatusDone</span>
            <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Printf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Completed task %d: %s</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ID</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Title</span><span class="p">)</span>
            <span class="k">return</span> <span class="no">nil</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="k">return</span> <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Errorf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"task %d not found"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">delete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="k">mut</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">u32</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">-></span> <span class="nb">Result</span><span class="o"><</span><span class="p">(),</span> <span class="nb">String</span><span class="o">></span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">pos</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="py">.tasks</span><span class="nf">.iter</span><span class="p">()</span>    <span class="c1">// get an iterator</span>
        <span class="nf">.position</span><span class="p">(|</span><span class="n">t</span><span class="p">|</span> <span class="n">t</span><span class="py">.id</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">)</span>  <span class="c1">// find the index of the id</span>
        <span class="nf">.ok_or_else</span><span class="p">(||</span> 
            <span class="nd">format!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Task {} not found"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="p">)</span><span class="o">?</span><span class="p">;</span>

    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">removed</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="k">self</span><span class="py">.tasks</span><span class="nf">.remove</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">pos</span><span class="p">);</span>
    <span class="nd">println!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Removed task {}: {}"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">removed</span><span class="py">.id</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">removed</span><span class="py">.title</span><span class="p">);</span> 
    <span class="nf">Ok</span><span class="p">(())</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>The conventions between <strong>Rust</strong> and <strong>Go</strong> become very visibly noticeable in this manner of presentation.<br>
</p>

<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">case</span> <span class="s">"done"</span><span class="o">:</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">args</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="m">3</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Fprintln</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Stderr</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s">"Usage: taskr done <id>"</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="m">1</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="n">store</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Complete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">parseID</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="m">2</span><span class="p">]));</span> <span class="n">err</span> <span class="o">!=</span> <span class="no">nil</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Fprintln</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Stderr</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">err</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="m">1</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="n">mustSave</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">store</span><span class="p">)</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="s">"done"</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="nf">.len</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="mi">3</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="nd">eprintln!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Usage: taskr done"</span><span class="p">);</span>
        <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">process</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">);</span> <span class="c1">// SIGTERM 1</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">id</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">u32</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="nf">.parse</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="nf">.unwrap_or_else</span><span class="p">(|</span><span class="n">_</span><span class="p">|</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="nd">eprintln!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Invalid task ID: {}"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">]);</span>
        <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">process</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">);</span> <span class="c1">// SIGTERM 1</span>
    <span class="p">});</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="k">let</span> <span class="nf">Err</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">e</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">store</span><span class="nf">.complete</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">id</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="nd">eprintln!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"{}"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">e</span><span class="p">);</span>
        <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">process</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">);</span> <span class="c1">// SIGTERM 1</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="n">store</span><span class="nf">.save</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="o">?</span><span class="p">;</span>
    <span class="nf">Ok</span><span class="p">(())</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<h3>
  
  
  TaskStore's <code>search</code> Implemented
</h3>



<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">func</span> <span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">TaskStore</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="n">Search</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">query</span> <span class="kt">string</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">[]</span><span class="o">*</span><span class="n">Task</span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="n">queryLower</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="n">strings</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ToLower</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">query</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="k">var</span> <span class="n">results</span> <span class="p">[]</span><span class="o">*</span><span class="n">Task</span>
    <span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">i</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="k">range</span> <span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">strings</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Contains</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">strings</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ToLower</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Title</span><span class="p">),</span> <span class="n">queryLower</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="p">{</span>
            <span class="n">results</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="nb">append</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">results</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="o">&</span><span class="n">s</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">tasks</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="n">i</span><span class="p">])</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="k">return</span> <span class="n">results</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="k">fn</span> <span class="nf">search</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="k">self</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">query</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="o">&</span><span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">-></span> <span class="nb">Vec</span><span class="o"><&</span><span class="n">Task</span><span class="o">></span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">query_lower</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">query</span><span class="nf">.to_lowercase</span><span class="p">();</span>

    <span class="k">self</span><span class="py">.tasks</span><span class="nf">.iter</span><span class="p">()</span>                    <span class="c1">// get an iterator</span>
        <span class="nf">.filter</span><span class="p">(|</span><span class="n">task</span><span class="p">|</span>                   <span class="c1">// each task loop</span>
            <span class="n">task</span><span class="py">.title</span><span class="nf">.to_lowercase</span><span class="p">()</span>    <span class="c1">// get lowercase title</span>
                <span class="nf">.contains</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="n">query_lower</span><span class="p">)</span>  <span class="c1">// match with query_lower</span>
        <span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="nf">.collect</span><span class="p">()</span>                       <span class="c1">// gather into vector</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<p>Specifically, the <strong>Rust</strong> is returning <code>Vec<&Task></code> which is a referenced object explicitly from the <code>.collect()</code> output.<br>
</p>

<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight go"><code><span class="k">case</span> <span class="s">"search"</span><span class="o">:</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">args</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="m">3</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Fprintln</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Stderr</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s">"Usage: taskr search <query>"</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="n">os</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="m">1</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="n">query</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="n">strings</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Join</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="m">2</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="s">" "</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="n">results</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="n">store</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Search</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">query</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">results</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="m">0</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Printf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"No tasks found matching %q</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">query</span><span class="p">)</span>
    <span class="p">}</span> <span class="k">else</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Printf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Found %d tasks matching: %s</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">results</span><span class="p">),</span> <span class="n">query</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">_</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="k">range</span> <span class="n">results</span> <span class="p">{</span>
            <span class="n">marker</span> <span class="o">:=</span> <span class="s">"[ ]"</span>
            <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Status</span> <span class="o">==</span> <span class="n">StatusDone</span> <span class="p">{</span>
                <span class="n">marker</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="s">"[X]"</span>
            <span class="p">}</span>
            <span class="n">fmt</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Printf</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"%s %d: %s</span><span class="se">\n</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">marker</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">ID</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">Title</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>





<div class="highlight js-code-highlight">
<pre class="highlight rust"><code><span class="s">"search"</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="p">{</span>
    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="nf">.len</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="o"><</span> <span class="mi">3</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="nd">eprintln!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Usage: taskr search <query>"</span><span class="p">);</span>
        <span class="nn">std</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nn">process</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="nf">exit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">);</span> <span class="c1">// SIGTERM 1</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">query</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">args</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="o">..</span><span class="p">]</span><span class="nf">.join</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">" "</span><span class="p">);</span> 
    <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">results</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">store</span><span class="nf">.search</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="o">&</span><span class="n">query</span><span class="p">);</span>

    <span class="k">if</span> <span class="n">results</span><span class="nf">.is_empty</span><span class="p">()</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="nd">println!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"No tasks found matching </span><span class="se">\"</span><span class="s">{}</span><span class="se">\"</span><span class="s">"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">query</span><span class="p">);</span>
    <span class="p">}</span> <span class="k">else</span> <span class="p">{</span>
        <span class="nd">println!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"Found {} tasks matching: {}"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">results</span><span class="nf">.len</span><span class="p">(),</span> <span class="n">query</span><span class="p">);</span>
        <span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">task</span> <span class="k">in</span> <span class="o">&</span><span class="n">results</span> <span class="p">{</span> 
            <span class="k">let</span> <span class="n">marker</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="k">match</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="py">.status</span> <span class="p">{</span> 
                <span class="nn">Status</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="n">Todo</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="s">"[ ]"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="c1">// incomplete items</span>
                <span class="nn">Status</span><span class="p">::</span><span class="n">Done</span> <span class="k">=></span> <span class="s">"[X]"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="c1">// completed items</span>
            <span class="p">};</span>
            <span class="nd">println!</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s">"{} {}: {}"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">marker</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="py">.id</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">task</span><span class="py">.title</span><span class="p">);</span>
        <span class="p">}</span>
    <span class="p">}</span>

    <span class="nf">Ok</span><span class="p">(())</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</code></pre>

</div>



<h2>
  
  
  Conclusion
</h2>

<p>You can view the <a href="https://gist.github.com/andreimerlescu/82332b73f37fdc45b99ce5ea8e909c8b" rel="noopener noreferrer">Go</a> and <a href="https://gist.github.com/andreimerlescu/1e807d841aceb8d73ca32b9309895b35" rel="noopener noreferrer">Rust</a> full source code respectively at either link provided. </p>

<p>This tutorial was designed to help bring <strong>Go</strong> engineers over to <strong>Rust</strong> by completing a command line task manager application in both languages to see how they are both the <em>similar</em> and <em>different</em>. </p>

<p>Are you coming into <strong>Rust</strong> from <strong>Go</strong> like I am? If so, what are some of your favorite resources to use and learn from?</p>
